name: Turmeric Phytosome
historicalUse: Turmeric phytosome is a modern bioavailability-enhanced form of turmeric curcuminoids. For historical use, see the Turmeric Root Extract monograph. The phytosome technology — binding curcuminoids to phosphatidylcholine — was developed in Italy by Indena S.p.A. to address the notoriously poor oral bioavailability of standard curcumin extracts, a limitation that may have reduced the efficacy of classical curcumin-rich preparations.
classicalSources: See Turmeric Root Extract monograph for full historical context
modernEvidence: Meriva® (curcumin-phosphatidylcholine phytosome) demonstrates 29-fold greater bioavailability than standard 95% curcuminoid extract in comparative pharmacokinetic studies. Multiple clinical trials using Meriva® specifically have shown significant improvements in joint pain, mobility, and inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6) in osteoarthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, and post-exercise recovery. The phospholipid matrix facilitates lymphatic absorption, bypassing hepatic first-pass metabolism.
mechanisms: Superior bioavailability — phospholipid complex enables 29x greater curcuminoid absorption
Lymphatic uptake pathway — phytosome matrix facilitates absorption via intestinal lymphatics
NF-kB inhibition — same anti-inflammatory pathway as standard curcumin at lower doses
COX-2 and 5-LOX inhibition — reduces prostaglandin and leukotriene synthesis
Nrf2 activation — upregulates endogenous antioxidant defense systems
benefits: Supports healthy joint comfort and mobility
Supports balanced inflammatory response with superior bioavailability
Contributes to post-exercise recovery and tissue comfort
Supports antioxidant defense at effective serum concentrations
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. Meriva® is the most clinically studied phytosome form. No significant adverse effects in clinical trials. Preferred over standard curcumin in patients with absorption concerns.
contraindications: Soy allergy — phosphatidylcholine may be soy-derived; verify source
drugInteractions: Anticoagulants — additive antiplatelet effect; monitor INR
Chemotherapy agents — consult oncologist; curcumin may modulate efficacy
CYP3A4 substrates — high-dose curcuminoids may inhibit CYP3A4
physicianNotes: Meriva® is the preferred clinical form of curcumin for conditions requiring measurable serum curcuminoid levels. The 29-fold bioavailability advantage means effective clinical doses are achievable without the very high gram-level doses needed with standard extracts. Specify phospholipid source (soy vs sunflower) for patients with soy allergy.
patientGuidance: This is an advanced form of turmeric that your body can absorb much more effectively than standard turmeric supplements. Take with a meal containing some fat for best results.
